授業目的: 旧石器時代から室町時代までの日本美術史を勉強し、美術作品の見方と説明の仕方を練習します。その時代の芸術と今のメディアやポップカルチャーとの関係を探る。
到達目標: 日本美術の作品の分析と説明をより詳しくできるようになります。
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（美術Ｂ）（CIEC）/Japanese Art B（国際学部）
関連科目: 総合日本学習科目（美術A）
授業方法: Lectures, Class and Small Group Discussions, Student Presentations

第1回:
・Startup Week
・Online class（On-demand format)
・《50minutes》
・URL: https://kwansei.jp.panopto.com/Panopto/Pages/Viewer.aspx?id=e11c4551-d2a4-4487-8c59-b42000b60692
PREPARE: Please be prepared to introduce yourself and to discuss the content of the on-demand video during our first core week face-to-face class.
第2回:
READ: Reynolds, T. E. G. "The Early Palaeolithic of Japan." Antiquity 59.226 (1985): 93-96
READ: Hudson, Mark J. “For the People, by the People: Postwar Japanese Archaeology and the Early Paleolithic Hoax,” Asian Perspectives, vol. 49, no. 2, 2005, pp. 131 – 139
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 1 (details provided in class)
第3回:
READ: “Japan, 8000–2000 B.C.” In Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History. New York: The Metropolitan Museum of Art, 2000–
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japan, 2000 – 1000 B.C.,” The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2000
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 2
第4回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Jomon Culture,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Dogu with Goggles excavated in Kamegaoka site, Aomori prefecture (Shakoki Dogu),” by NHK World
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 3
第5回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japan, 1000 B.C. – 1 A.D.,” The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2000
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japan, 1 – 500 A.D.,” The Metropolitan Museum of Art, 2000
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Yayoi Culture,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Ritual Bell with Crossed Band Design (Dotaku),” by NHK World, April 16, 2015
PREPARE:Research Portfolio Entry 4
第6回:
Independent research for Annotated Bibliography assignment
・Flex Hours 
・Online class（Task-oriented format）
・《100minutes》
RESEARCH: Your chosen annotated bibliography topic. Search for useful primary, secondary, and tertiary sources using web databases and university library services.
第7回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Kofun Period,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Daryukyo – Bronze Mirror with Design of Divinities and Dragons,” by NHK World, October 12, 2017
LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Haniwa (Terracotta Tomb Figurine), Warrior In Keiko Armor (Haniwa Keiko no Bujin),” by NHK World
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 5
第8回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japan, 500 – 1000 A.D.,” The Metropolitan Museum of Art, 2000
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Internationalism in the Tang Dynasty,” by Heather Colburn Clydesdale, April 2009
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Asuka and Nara Periods,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
READ/LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Carved Wood Gigaku Mask of Konron (Gigaku Men Konron),” by NHK World
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 6
第9回:
Sign-up for Optional Research Presentations
Core Week 07
・Face-to-face class
・《90minutes》

READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Buddhism and Buddhist Art,” by Vidya Dehejia, Department of Art History and Archaeology, Columbia University, February 2007
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Shinto,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japanese Incense,” by Monica Bincsik, Department of Asian Art, Metropolitan Museum of Art, March 2009
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 7
第10回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Heian Period,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Kings of Brightness in Esoteric Buddhist Art,” by Sinead Vilbar, October 2013
READ/LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Fugen Bosatsu (Samantabhadra Bodhisattva) (Fugen Bosatsu zo),” by NHK World
PREPARE: Research Portfolio Entry 8
第11回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Yamato-e Painting,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2003
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Japanese Illustrated Handscrolls,” by Anna Willmann, Department of Asian Art, Metropolitan Museum of Art, November 2012
READ: Watanabe, Masako. “The Art of Japanese Storytelling.” Storytelling in Japanese Art, pp. 3 – 47, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, 2011
READ/LISTEN: “The Magic of Japanese Masterpieces, Narrative Picture Scroll of the Chronicle of the Heiji Civil War: The Removal of the Imperial Family to Rokuhara (Heiji Monogatari Emaki, Rokuhara Gyoukou no Maki),” by NHK World
READ/VIEW: “Heiji Scroll – Introduction,” Bowdoin College, 2010
第12回:
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Kamakura & Nanbokucho Periods,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Samurai,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Shoguns and Art,” the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2004
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, The Japanese Blade: Technology and Manufacture,” by Edward Hunter, Department of Arms and Armor, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2003
READ: “Heilbrunn Timeline of Art History, Zen Buddhism,” by the Department of Asian Art, The Metropolitan Museum of Art, October 2002
第13回:
COMPLETE: Annotated Bibliography
COMPLETE: (For volunteers only) Optional Research Presentations. Presentation length and date subject to change according to the number of volunteers. Details to be announced in class.

Optional Research Presentations (presentation length and date subject to change according to number of participants; details to be announced in class)

教科書/Required texts0: All materials will be provided by the lecturer.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic:
各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。
DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。
各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。
https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1
※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）
Feedback will be provided during class and by email upon request.

Engagement with lectures, participation in class and small group discussions (42%), preparation for small group discussions (8%)

Late submissions of assignments will not be accepted, unless under the previously mentioned circumstances.
